# Native Select

A styled native select that preserves browser and platform selection behavior.

## Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [Controlled values](#controlled-values)
- [Options and groups](#options-and-groups)
- [Validation](#validation)
- [Accessibility](#accessibility)
- [Choosing a picker](#choosing-a-picker)

## Installation

Add Native Select from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/native-select
```

## Usage

Use Native Select for a straightforward single choice when native mobile pickers, keyboard behavior, autofill, and ordinary form submission are valuable. Render `NativeSelectOption` and optional `NativeSelectOptGroup` children directly inside it.

**Grouped options example**

```vue
<template>
  <NativeSelect class="w-full max-w-xs">
    <NativeSelectOption value="">Select a department</NativeSelectOption>
    <NativeSelectOptGroup label="Engineering">
      <NativeSelectOption value="frontend">Frontend</NativeSelectOption>
      <NativeSelectOption value="backend">Backend</NativeSelectOption>
      <NativeSelectOption value="devops">DevOps</NativeSelectOption>
    </NativeSelectOptGroup>
    <NativeSelectOptGroup label="Operations">
      <NativeSelectOption value="support">Customer support</NativeSelectOption>
      <NativeSelectOption value="product">Product management</NativeSelectOption>
    </NativeSelectOptGroup>
  </NativeSelect>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import {
  NativeSelect,
  NativeSelectOptGroup,
  NativeSelectOption,
} from '@/Components/NativeSelect'
</script>
```

## Controlled values

Bind `v-model` when the chosen value affects application state. Without a bound model, Native Select keeps its own value and can submit it by `name` with a native form. The local model accepts a scalar or array, but use a scalar for the normal single-select case and add the native `multiple` attribute only when the browser's multi-select interaction is genuinely appropriate.

## Options and groups

Give each option a stable value and use an optgroup label to name a meaningful category. A placeholder option usually uses an empty value; mark it disabled when users must not return to the empty state. Disable individual unavailable options rather than the whole select when other valid choices remain.

## Validation

Native Select forwards attributes to the underlying `select`. Use `required` for native constraints, `aria-invalid` after validation fails, and a nearby message that explains how to recover. The wrapper detects the disabled state, while the select itself owns focus and invalid styling.

**Controlled invalid state example**

```vue
<template>
  <Field class="w-full max-w-xs" data-invalid>
    <FieldLabel for="native-select-country">Country</FieldLabel>
    <NativeSelect
      id="native-select-country"
      v-model="country"
      aria-invalid
      name="country"
      required
    >
      <NativeSelectOption value="">Select a country</NativeSelectOption>
      <NativeSelectOption value="at">Austria</NativeSelectOption>
      <NativeSelectOption value="cz">Czechia</NativeSelectOption>
      <NativeSelectOption value="sk">Slovakia</NativeSelectOption>
    </NativeSelect>
    <FieldError>Select a country.</FieldError>
  </Field>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Field, FieldError, FieldLabel } from '@/Components/Field'
import {
  NativeSelect,
  NativeSelectOption,
} from '@/Components/NativeSelect'
import { ref } from 'vue'

const country = ref('')
</script>
```

## Accessibility

Preserve a visible label linked to the select id. Native options and optgroups already expose platform semantics, so avoid placing arbitrary interactive content inside them. The decorative chevron is hidden from assistive technology.

## Choosing a picker

> **Use Select for a custom popup: [Select](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/select)**
>
> Use Select when the interface needs consistent custom popup styling, grouped separators, or richer option presentation and the list remains easy to scan.

> **Use Combobox for a searchable list: [Combobox](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/combobox)**
>
> When the option set is long or users benefit from typing to filter it, use Combobox instead of forcing them to scan a native picker.

> **Use Radio Group for a small visible set: [Radio Group](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/radio-group)**
>
> If there are only a few important choices and comparison matters, show them all with Radio Group rather than hiding them in a menu.

Hosted documentation: [Native Select](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/native-select)
